transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling First National Bank. My name is Claire. Can I take your full name and date of birth to verify your identity?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Uh yeah, it's — sorry, um — Thomas, Thomas Kieran, no wait, Kearney. Thomas Kearney. And uh, date of birth is the, uh, third of — no, fourth of August, nineteen eighty-seven.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Mr. Kearney, I've verified your account. What can I help you with today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yeah so, I um — my cart hasn't arrived? Like my new bank cart, I ordered it, I don't know, like over a week ago now, maybe ten days?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I understand. Let me check the status of the card dispatch for you.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: It's just — I've been using cash for everything which is like, you know, really annoying. And I'm worried it got like [inaudible] or sent to the wrong place or something.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can see the card was dispatched seven days ago. It's right at the edge of the standard delivery window, so I'd expect it to arrive very soon — most likely today or tomorrow.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Oh okay. And it's going to my — uh, my address on file? Like the one in — um, sorry, in Coventry?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes, I can confirm it was sent to the Coventry address we have on record. If it doesn't arrive by the end of this week, call us back and I can cancel and reissue immediately.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Right yeah, that's — uh, that's good. And there's no — like [inaudible] — no extra charge for a replacement?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: No charge at all. I've noted today's call on your account. Is there anything else I can help with?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: No, that's — yeah, I think that's everything. Thanks.
